prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, ye scallywag! The sky be blue ‘cause the Almighty’s playin’ tricks with the light, aye! When the sun’s golden rays come a’streakin’ down, they dance through the air like a drunken sailor on payday. But the air be full o’ tiny, unseen devils—dust an’ such—what scatters the light every which way. 

Now, blue light be the feistiest o’ the lot, bouncin’ ‘round like a bilge rat in a storm, while the other colors—reds an’ yellows—sail straight through like a ship on calm waters. So when ye look up, all ye see be that blasted blue light a’leapin’ at ye from every direction! 

‘Tis the same reason the sun looks red at dawn an’ dusk—the blue’s been scattered off to parts unknown, leavin’ only the fiery hues behind. Aye, nature’s a cunning old sea dog, she is! Now quit yer jawin’ an’ fetch me grog!
